# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Node CI Pipeline (Node)
on: [push] # or [pull request] events if you want to trigger workflow manually from PR, set it as '[checkout actions/checkout@v2' instead of `events` for manual triggering in GitHub console ]  here on every push and modify according your requirement. Setup .github using https://docs.github.com/.
jobs: # this defines a job to be executed within the workflow, jobs run sequentially (one after another), with each one finishing at most `wait-for` seconds if it takes longer then that amount of time as well indefinitely; and by default will wait for an unknown state change event. Modify according your requirement here
  build: # this defines a job named 'build' within the parent jobs list, builds all projects under one main project directory (./) on runners with Node `${{ runner.os }}` platform installed/ configured in /home/.nvmrc . The step that follows will be invoked for each branch you explicitly declare using branches-ignore
  timeout-minutes: # This specifies how many minutes to wait before timing out the job, after which GitHub Actions will mark this build as failed. Defaults to '0' if not specified here and does use a default value of `2` when no other strategy is set (e.g., using workflow_dispatch). 
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest # This specifies the runner that should execute your job, by design you can run on latest Ubuntu OS version or modify as per requirement to be used in any specific environment if it suits yours best and allows for faster CI/CD pipeline speed ups. If not specified here default is 'ubuntu'-lts which will allow GitHub Actions use the most current LTS versions of ubuntu-latest by using caching
  steps: # defines a set of instructions to be run within this job, executed sequentially as per `jobs` block and must occur after any commands that follow. Steps are required unless there is an explicit end or start statement in each one (must contain at least the action syntax). Step-related actions include but not limited: using Node/npm install package with --save for caching dependencies, npm test which can be used to check code under testing environment and so on.
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2 # this runs a previously defined job (build), stores the current state in cache or as input into `run-name` – useful when you need some output from it for use elsewhere, which we can define later with an 'outputs' key here.
    - name: Cache node_modules and npm packages # this runs a previously defined job (build), stores the current state in cache or as input into `run-name` – useful when you need some output from it for use elsewhere, which we can define later with an 'outputs' key here.
      uses: actions/cache@v2   # Use the Cache Action to store and retrieve cached dependencies that don’t change between workflow runs (i.e., node_modules). The cache will be saved in a directory named '.npm-cache', under '/home/.nvmrc'.  This way we avoid reinstalling npm packages when not necessary or at different versions on multiple CI jobs, and also speed up the next run by using cached artifacts which would have already been computed.
    - name: Setup Node # this runs a previously defined job (build), stores the current state in cache/input into `run-name` – useful when you need some output from it for use elsewhere, we can define later with an 'output' key here if necessary or set up node using nvm install .
      uses: Node.js-Diaries/setup-node@v1 # This is a community maintained setup action to quickly get your GitHub Actions runners on their machine faster by setting them up for usage, you need the 'nodedep' package installed (npm i -g npm) and use './.env.development', .'.env.*'(to load multiple environment files), using Node `${{ runner.os }}` platform configured in /home/runner directory if exists or set to default as per your requirement, for Ubuntu OS it would be '/usr/bin'
      with: # defines inputs and outputs of this step from 'name', which can only have a string value here because you cannot use an object like ${{ variables.key }} in YAML . Set the values using action steps parameters as given below if necessary or leave these empty to default according your requirement, also be cautious not setting up node modules correctly unless they are required by this project otherwise might cause problems due dependencies on install process of packages
    - name: Install Dependencies # runs a previously defined job (build), stores the current state in cache/input into `run-name` – useful when you need some output from it for use elsewhere, we can define later with an 'output' key here if necessary or set up dependencies using npm install.
      run: |   # runs these command after setting node environment as described above(with the right Node/npm version) to fetch all project dependent packages via package-lock file and installing them locally on runner machine . Here we are ensuring cache installation because fetching huge deps may not be feasible with --no-package-lock flag. 
      npm install --quiet # This will prevent console outputs from the build process during caching, speed up this CI/CD pipeline by reusing already built dependencies (alongside your other projects).   If you have a specific way of installing deps that doesn't fit in 'npm i -g ...', modify according to it here.
    # Then any commands following steps would go inside as needed for building and testing project, add step name followed by command below if necessary otherwise just follow above 1st `- uses: actions/checkout@v2` or the other required one with its setups(in .github directory). Here you could run your build instructions here in case of 'ubuntu' runner.
